1.11: Dear friend, don't follow bad examples—follow good ones. Those who do good belong to God; those who do evil have never known God.
1.1: I, the elder, am writing to my dear friend Gaius, whom I love in truth.
1.6: They've told the whole church about your love. Please help them on their journey in a way that is worthy of God.
1.2: Dear friend, I hope everything is going well with you and that you are in good health—just as you are thriving spiritually.
1.5: Dear friend, you are doing a faithful work by caring for the traveling believers, even those you've never met.
1.10: If I come, I will expose what he's doing—spreading lies about us. And on top of that, he refuses to welcome the visiting believers. He even tries to stop those who want to welcome them and throws them out of the church!
1.3: I was overjoyed when fellow believers came and told me how faithfully you are walking in the truth.
1.4: Nothing brings me more joy than hearing that my spiritual children are walking in the truth.
1.12: Everyone speaks well of Demetrius—the truth itself commends him. And so do I—you know my testimony is reliable.
1.8: We have a responsibility to support people like these—so that we can be fellow workers with the truth.
1.13: I have much more to say, but I don't want to put it all in writing.
1.9: I wrote to the church, but Diotrephes—who loves to be in charge—won't have anything to do with us.
1.7: They set out to serve God and won't accept any support from unbelievers.
1.14: I hope to see you soon, and then we can talk in person. Peace to you! The friends here send their love. Please greet each of the friends there by name.
